g.text: CAP, properly stated: during a partition, pick one — not always two of three
When the network is healthy, both Consistency and Availability are achievable. The CAP forced-choice happens only at the moment a partition begins.
⚡ PARTITION starts here
(network splits the cluster)
Healthy network
(no partition)
✓ Consistency AND ✓ Availability
Every write reaches every replica.
Every read can be served and returns the latest value.
No trade-off is required — physics is not asking the question.
time →
Partition in progress: now you must pick one
CP — keep Consistency, lose Availability
Minority partition refuses reads + writes.
Majority quorum stays linearizable.
Single committed value everywhere when partition heals.
Cost: clients on the wrong side see downtime.
AP — keep Availability, lose Consistency
Both sides accept reads + writes.
Replicas diverge until the partition heals.
App must reconcile conflicts (LWW, CRDTs, vector clocks).
Cost: divergent state; conflict resolution becomes the app's job.
PACELC fills the gap: even without a partition (E), every system trades Latency for Consistency.
if Partition → C or A; Else → Latency or Consistency. PACELC says: tell me what you do in both worlds.
